status: design plan + scaffold; no code, no raw downloads
purpose: Population skeleton for the simulator: one row per represented voting-eligible adult/cell across 2008/2012/2016/2020/2024.
framing_principle: CES/CCES gives node signatures; ACS/PUMS gives the full eligible-adult population skeleton.
explicitly_not_in_v0: No PRISM node signatures — Phase 2.6 mapper handles signatures separately.
No vote prediction or candidate scoring — Phase 2.8+.
No raw ACS/PUMS downloads — gated behind explicit user approval and a separate intake commit (~10–15 GB across 5 vintages).
No Esri Tapestry use — pre-2020 vintages unavailable; downstream-of-ACS anyway.
No commercial voter files — only cover ~70% of adults (registered voters); abstention calibration needs full eligible universe.
No mapper / vote-formula / candidate / EIG / selector / browser / dist / output edits.

cell_marginals.role: Reproducible demographic totals at any geography for raking / IPF
cell_marginals.produces: Cells of (year, state, age_bucket, race, sex, education, income_bucket, citizenship) with population counts

individual_microdata.role: Synthetic-respondent rows with per-respondent uncertainty + survey-style replicate weights
individual_microdata.produces: One row per PUMS respondent, with personWeight + 80 replicate weights

fields.name: state
fields.notes: 50 states + DC; territories excluded for v1

fields.name: sex
fields.notes: PUMS does not include non-binary 2008-2024 5-year files

fields.name: vepEligible
fields.notes: true iff citizenship ∈ {us_born, us_naturalized} AND age >= 18 AND not in disqualifying state-level felon-disenfranchisement category

fields.name: personWeight
fields.notes: Population share carried by this row

fields.name: replicateWeights
fields.notes: 80 replicate weights for variance estimation; optional consumption

fields.name: uncertainty
fields.notes: low for state aggregates; medium at PUMA; high when small cells get IPF/raking smoothing

target_row_schema.join_key_for_simulator: respondentId; the synthetic electorate is the union of all rows where vepEligible === true, weighted by personWeight

source_files_per_year.pums_file: configured IPUMS extract (5-year preferred for sub-state coverage; supplement with 2008 1-year for state-level totals where 1-year supports the geography)
source_files_per_year.notes: ACS 5-year coverage starts 2009; for 2008 specifically, 2005-2009 5-year is the workhorse.
2020 1-year ACS NOT released (COVID nonresponse); 5-year is the only ACS option.
If 2020-2024 not yet shipped, use 2019-2023 with documented vintage_lag: 1 flag.

v1_acquisition_plan.status: gated; requires explicit user approval before downloads
steps: 1. Open IPUMS USA account (free; institutional or personal).
2. Configure five extracts (one per cycle), each containing all 50 states + DC and all PUMAs.
3. Required variables per extract: YEAR, STATEFIP, PUMA, SERIALNO, SPORDER, PWGTP, PWGTP1..80, AGEP, SEX, RAC1P, HISP, SCHL, HINCP, CIT, MIGSP1.
4. Subset to age 18+ to halve file size.
v1_acquisition_plan.until_acquisition_lands: Loader v1 will return a typed 'no data available for year X' sentinel rather than throwing.

loader_output_contract.load_export: loadVepUniverse(opts): Promise<{ rows: VepUniverseRow[]; stats: VepUniverseStats; }>
stats_fields: benchmarkComparison: { vepGapShare: number; vepGapAbsolute: number; benchmarkSource: 'mcdonald_uf' }

validation_invariants: Total weighted VEP per year matches benchmark within 1.5% (allows for felon-disenfranchisement methodology differences).
State-level VEP totals match McDonald/UF state breakdowns within 2% per state.
Age-by-citizenship marginals match ACS B05003 within ACS sampling error (90% CI overlap).
All vepEligible: true rows have age >= 18 and citizenship ∈ {us_born, us_naturalized}.
Replicate weights present and finite for every row.

felon_disenfranchisement.issue: PUMS does not include conviction history; McDonald/UF VEP already applies a felon-disenfranchisement adjustment.
felon_disenfranchisement.v1_approach: Apply a static per-state percentage from the Sentencing Project's tracker. vepEligible becomes a probabilistic flag (e.g., 97% true / 3% false) for affected demographic cells.
felon_disenfranchisement.v2_approach: Replace static percentages with a year-specific tracker table.
felon_disenfranchisement.audit_signal: Loader's benchmarkComparison.vepGap surfaces any residual mismatch between PUMS-derived VEP and McDonald/UF VEP.

voter_files.rationale_against: Cover only ~70% of adults (registered voters). Abstention calibration (Phase 2.4 Stage B) integrates over full eligible universe; need unregistered-but-eligible adults too.
voter_files.future_role: Possible cross-validation of turnout subgroup behavior at v3+; not the universe definition.

esri_tapestry.rationale_against_universe: Census-derivative; using it as the universe layer would be circular vs ACS used directly. Plus pre-2020 vintages unavailable.
esri_tapestry.future_role: Optional MRP covariate or narrative coloring at reporting time. Not the population skeleton.

decennial_only.rationale_against: Decadal cadence is too coarse for cycle-by-cycle backtest; each election year needs ACS 5-year ending at or before that year.

depends_on: Phase 2.2 — benchmarks-2008-2024 (read-only consumption for VEP cross-check)
Phase 2.4 — turnout-abstention-calibration-plan (Stage B abstention will integrate over this universe)
Phase 2.6 — survey-to-prism mapper (signatures will be imputed onto this universe by demographic cell)
blocks: Phase 2.8 — mapper-backed aggregate target requires this universe as denominator for share-of-VEP comparisons.

open_issues_for_v1: Felon-disenfranchisement state lookup table — Sentencing Project tracker URL + retrieval cadence.
Whether to ship MRP raking helper inside the loader or as a separate module (prefer separate; mapper does not need raking for v1 backtest).
PUMA-2010 vs PUMA-2020 boundary changes — pre-2018 PUMS uses 2010 boundaries; 2018+ uses 2020 boundaries. Cross-vintage analyses need a crosswalk.
MIGSP1 (residence 1 year ago) — extract now for future migration-model use, but loader v1 does not consume it.
terminology: vepEligible is the population-skeleton invariant; engagement and turnout are downstream concerns. Engagement is referenced only as the input to Stage B abstention calibration once the universe is loaded — separate 1D continuous scalar per ADR canon. Compound moral-circle (moralBoundaries) terminology used where applicable. Legacy code identifiers (PWGTP, RAC1P, SCHL etc.) appear only as PUMS column names.
